deck.title: MRO Market Sizing — Surface Vessel Opportunities

placeholders.title: US Surface Vessel MRO Market Sizing

slides.intent: executive overview — context + objectives for MRO sizing
MRO archetype comparison — ASV vs NSV across 5 attributes

placeholders.title: MRO archetypes | Sizing covers ASV sustainment plus NSV MRO across Navy, USCG, and small commercial — bracketing total yard demand.
attributes: Why we are sized for it
placeholders.source: Source: PB26 budget exhibits; CRS RL32665; USCG FY26 budget-in-brief; HII and Fincantieri 10-Ks

slides.intent: FY26 TAM hero by vessel-class segment

placeholders.title: FY26 TAM | ~$8.5-10.4B across surface MRO, with NSV manned-capability accounting for ~95% and ASV sustainment the remaining ~5%.
placeholders.chart_description: FY26 surface MRO TAM by vessel-class segment ($B): DDG/CG, FFG/LCS, auxiliaries, USCG cutters, small commercial, ASV
placeholders.source: Source: NVR; PB26 O&M-N exhibits; USCG FY26 budget; OBBBA USV appropriations

placeholders.title: Yard-accessible SAM | $1.5-2.0B p.a. is reachable from a single Gulf-Coast yard, growing as ASV sustainment ramps and NSV availabilities shift to overflow yards.
placeholders.chart_description: Yard-accessible SAM by archetype, FY26-FY28 ($M): ASV MRO, NSV Navy, NSV USCG + commercial

rationale_bullets.header: Why SAM is well below TAM
bullets: ASV fleet ~120 hulls FY26 grows to ~280 by FY28

placeholders.conclusion_text: Yard sized for FY28 SAM of ~$2.0B.
placeholders.source: Source: NAVSEA RMC backlog; HII and Fincantieri 10-Ks; Saronic yard model

slides.intent: Company TCV / ACV chart with implied-volume table below

placeholders.title: Company TCV to ACV | TCV grows from ~$610M in FY26 to ~$1.06B in FY28; ACV ramps from ~$180M to ~$410M as the yard reaches steady-state throughput.
placeholders.chart_description: Company TCV ($M) FY26-FY28 by archetype: ASV sustainment, NSV Navy availabilities, NSV USCG + commercial
placeholders.source: Source: NAVSEA contract awards (FPDS-NG, FY24-26 YTD); RMC backlog reports; internal pricing model

slides.intent: Vessel triangulation cumulative chart + next-steps commentary

placeholders.title: Vessel triangulation | Assumptions imply the yard touches ~88 NSV availabilities and ~660 ASV sustainment events through FY28.
placeholders.chart_description: Cumulative vessel-touch events through end-of-FY: ASV sustainment, NSV availabilities, modernization periods

bullets: ASV events drive ~30% of FTE hours by FY28
ASV sustainment lower margin, higher volume
MARMC overflow award is the swing factor

placeholders.conclusion_text: Pursue MASC, lock ASV pricing, anchor with modernization.
placeholders.source: Source: NVR; FPDS-NG FY22-26 YTD; RMC backlog; Saronic yard and pricing model
